mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in a multivariate regression over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving corporate output capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative returns in >55% of recovery regime periods, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.02 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of rising payroll periods, or the rank correlation between payroll growth and 20-day returns remains below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production reflect synchronized demand and supply expansion, reducing inventory bottlenecks and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of synchronized expansion periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3% annualized confirms strong macroeconomic momentum, boosting corporate profit expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >60% of high-GDP growth quarters, or the signal information coefficient decays below 0.02 within 12 months of regime onset.
